note: Advisory sandbox batch linter. Emits CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED verdicts, never a bare governance PASS. Read-only over the P0-PGR outbox; reuses the ground per-packet linter unforked. The tool itself is the trust spine.
title: Batch Outbox Linter (static, read-only, over receipts/p0pgr/outbox/*.json)

subject.note: imports the ground per-packet linter unforked and runs its lint_packet() over each of the 10 outbox packets; all files read-only, sha unchanged

finding.interpretation: All 10 real outbox packets lint clean (ground linter verdict PASS each). The batch gate reports CHECK_OK honestly and exits 0. Unlike the ground linter — which is bound by RUNTIME_CONTINUITY_LAW_v1 to always exit 0 so a malformed packet never freezes the lane — MO-5 exits NONZERO the moment any packet is a REPAIR_CANDIDATE, making it usable as a pre-flight batch gate.

what_it_proves: REAL outbox (10 packets) -> CHECK_OK, exit 0: every packet lints PASS, reported honestly as the true verdict (whole-set positive control, not a stub)

a MINIMAL MUTATION of ONE real packet (drop the required `fallback_route` key) in a TEMP COPY flips that packet to REPAIR_CANDIDATE and the batch to CHECK_REJECTED, exit 1; the other 9 stay PASS (RED-before-GREEN, seed file cited)
a SECOND independent minimal mutation (cloud_safe=false on a CLOUD_ONLY packet) also flips the batch -> the gate is not keyed to one lucky field; a broken-regex false-clean would miss at least one
positive control: an unmutated byte-copy of the real outbox stays CHECK_OK -> the rejection is caused by the mutation, not by copying
running MO-5 leaves every real outbox packet AND scripts/p0pgr_packet_lint_v1.py byte-identical (sha unchanged); strictly read-only
verdict vocab CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED, never a bare governance PASS; per-packet lint_verdict is the ground linter's own PASS/REPAIR_CANDIDATE classification, reported verbatim
chess_patches_applied: runs against the REAL outbox and records its true verdict (CHECK_OK); no strawman, no manufactured violation
red-capability proven by MINIMAL MUTATION of a real on-disk packet in a temp copy (no relaxation of the ground linter's rules — the same lint_packet() decides)
imports the ground linter unforked so a green batch actually exercises the live lint logic, not a re-implementation
reads packets + ground linter, never writes to either (Lock 5 / read-only); summary printed to stdout, optional --out confined to the build dir
origin=sandbox-advisory / authority=none; reports the batch verdict as a finding, never its own PASS
locks_respected: Lock 5 append-only (10 outbox packets + ground linter unmodified, sha unchanged; writes only inside catalog/builds/MO-5/)
Lock 1/2 no live call (no network, no Supabase, no send/dispatch; pure static read)
DRAFT-only: no dispatch triggered, queue untouched, no AUTO_DISPATCH_APPROVED
read-only batch linter; worktree not pushed; touched nothing outside the build dir
exit_gate_B3: MO-5 done (batch outbox linter reuses the ground per-packet linter unforked, reports the real outbox as CHECK_OK honestly, and is red-capable via minimal-mutation of a real packet + a second independent mutation as positive control).
next_action: If any future outbox packet lints REPAIR_CANDIDATE, MO-5 exits nonzero and cites the packet+reasons; wire it as a pre-dispatch batch gate over receipts/p0pgr/outbox/.
